campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule, approving plans for a new bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, and applied for grant money to help build it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship fund from Water Tower Kitchen
Extending interim ordinance #2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submitted for bridge construction funding

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide which organizations and events would receive city grant money for the upcoming fiscal year. They approved funding amounts for different groups, set up rules for a competitive grant program where events can apply for money, and clarified the rules for community events that want to close streets in Saratoga.
keyTopics: Approved specific dollar amounts for selected grant recipients
Established competitive grant program so local events can apply for funding
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants to make them clearer
Determined total budget available for event funding this fiscal year

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple platforms including cable TV, YouTube, Zoom, and phone. The agenda focused on making the meeting accessible to all residents, including those who need live translations in over 50 languages.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translation services available in 50+ languages
No public comment period scheduled for this meeting
Contact information and access links for residents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports and updates
Scheduled meetings for city boards and commissions
Access to meeting agendas and documents at City Clerk's Office

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund housing and community development projects in the city. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support affordable housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding available for local housing and community projects
Review of applications from organizations seeking CDBG money
Approval of February meeting records

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new initiatives. They discussed a program to help businesses get back on track with licensing requirements, plans to encourage people to shop at local stores and shopping centers, and ways to support small businesses and startups through incentives and community events.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help non-compliant businesses get back in good standing
Summer of Celebrations campaign to boost local shopping
Public-Private Partnerships to fund city events
Economic incentives for retail stores, small businesses, and startups
Outreach efforts to shopping centers across Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding a Governance and Ethics Committee meeting on March 30, 2026. The city is offering multiple ways for residents to attend—either in person, on Zoom, or by phone—so everyone can participate in local government discussions.
keyTopics: Committee meetings available both in-person and remotely
Zoom participation option (ID: 985 5995 1444)
Phone dial-in available at +1 669 444 9171
Public can submit written comments via email by 10 a.m. the day before

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several items including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council also approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software services costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
